CIPHER.291.cipherString: But naturally people fussed and objected like they always do when someone tries to do something positive.
CIPHER.291.question: Jane Rogers offers us some advice that has been encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.291.author: Jane Rogers

CIPHER.278.cipherString: I never wanted to be rich or successful or famous. I just wanted to be happy and have fun.
CIPHER.278.question: Someone passed you a piece of paper with this Checkerboard encoded quote by Donna Leon with a Polybius key of PROCLAIMS. What does it say?
CIPHER.278.author: Donna Leon

CIPHER.263.cipherString: Muchas personas no cuidan su dinero hasta que casi han llegado a su fin, y otras hacen lo mismo con su tiempo.
CIPHER.263.question: Etched into the bottom of the cafeteria's mystery-cookie tray: this Xenocrypt quote by Goethe in Spanish using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.263.author: Goethe
CIPHER.263.translation: Many people take no care of their money till they have come nearly to an end of it, and others do just the same with their time.

CIPHER.265.cipherString: Adversity is the state in which man most easily becomes acquainted with himself, being especially free of admirers then.
CIPHER.265.question: You came across the following written on a wall in a cave. You are told that it's a quote by John Wooden encoded as a Patristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains AD. What does it say?
CIPHER.265.author: John Wooden

CIPHER.285.cipherString: When one begins to search for general solutions, one's doubts about their utility are likely to grow stronger.
CIPHER.285.question: Found taped under a desk in study hall: this Aristocrat puzzle by Democracy and Its Critics using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Key Phrase used to encode it?
CIPHER.285.author: Democracy and It's Critics

CIPHER.282.question: You found the following carved into the bark of a hollow log. You recognize that it's encoded as a Cryptarithm. What do the values 48725 9736 1502 decode to?

CIPHER.277.cipherString: Well, it's very dangerous to project, but it's clear that the existing technology has some more years to go.
CIPHER.277.question: A mysterious app update added this Aristocrat lock screen—it's actually Jack Kilby's quote. You are told that the cipher contains IT'S twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.277.author: Jack Kilby

CIPHER.276.cipherString: Any achievement depends on diligence of the desire.
CIPHER.276.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Baconian encoded quote by Lailah Gifty Akita. What does it say?
CIPHER.276.author: Lailah Gifty Akita

CIPHER.273.cipherString: Nos divertimos sólo en nuestro trabajo, en nuestro hacer; Y lo mejor que hacemos es lo que más disfrutamos.
CIPHER.273.question: Someone taped a piece of paper with this sequence onto the back of a trophy from last year's Science Olympiad. It's a quote by Jacobi in Spanish, written using a Xenocrypt Cipher using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.273.author: Jacobi
CIPHER.273.translation: We enjoy ourselves only in our work, our doing; and our best doing is our best enjoyment.

CIPHER.272.cipherString: good news stops to take breath on the road ; bad news never requires it.
CIPHER.272.question: A weathered note is found inside an old science textbook. The writing appears to be a quote encoded as a Checkerboard with a Polybius key of DOWNSTREAM. What does it say?

CIPHER.262.cipherString: Friends, I have lost a day.
CIPHER.262.question: A quote by Emperor Titus has been encoded using the Hill Cipher with a keyword of BUNT for you to decode. What does it say?
CIPHER.262.author: Emperor Titus

CIPHER.269.cipherString: After one look at this planet any visitor from outer space would say I WANT TO SEE THE MANAGER.
CIPHER.269.question: While analyzing the background noise from a physics experiment, you detect this pattern. It's a quote, encoded using an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Key Phrase used to encode it?

CIPHER.286.cipherString: Pure and complete sorrow is as impossible as pure and complete joy.
CIPHER.286.question: A vending machine just dispensed a candy wrapper with this Nihilist Substitution code by Leo Tolstoy with a keyword of SIGNAL and Polybius key of ARGUMENTS. What does it say?
CIPHER.286.author: Leo Tolstoy

CIPHER.281.cipherString: One stumble is enough to deface the character of an honorable life.
CIPHER.281.question: This code is found written in chalk on the sidewalk outside the science building. It's a quote by L'Estrange, written in a Porta Cipher. The 41st through 47th cipher characters (RMWHFBA) decode to be ANHONOR. What does it say?
CIPHER.281.author: L'Estrange

CIPHER.280.cipherString: Your body's really only meant to compete at the highest levels of combat sports for a few years.
CIPHER.280.question: A mysterious QR code on the back of a cereal box leads to this encrypted quote by Joe Rogan using the Aristocrat Cipher with a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.280.author: Joe Rogan

CIPHER.270.question: Your drone photographed some weird markings in a parking lot. Turns out it's a quote in a Cryptarithm. What do the values 4012 67822 9255 1236 decode to?

CIPHER.267.cipherString: Everyone has a right to a university degree in America, even if it's in Hamburger Technology.
CIPHER.267.question: You discover a scrap of paper tucked inside an old lab notebook. It contains a quote by Clive James encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.267.author: Clive James

CIPHER.284.cipherString: I didn't get where I am today by worrying about how I'd feel tomorrow.
CIPHER.284.question: Archaeologists unearth a stone tablet inscribed with a message. It's a quote by Ron White encoded as a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of EASILY and Polybius key of NOTICEABLY. What does it say?
CIPHER.284.author: Ron White

CIPHER.288.cipherString: The unending quest of historians for understanding the past -- that is, revisionism -- is what makes history vital and meaningful.
CIPHER.288.question: A time-capsule USB drive from 1983 just auto-opened with this Patristocrat-encrypted message by James M. McPherson using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with THE. What does it say?
CIPHER.288.author: James M. McPherson

CIPHER.268.question: The cafeteria receipt printed a Proverb using the Hill cipher with a keyword of NUMBERING. What does it say?
CIPHER.268.author: Proverb

CIPHER.274.cipherString: I feel like honesty gives people the freedom to talk about things they wouldn't otherwise.
CIPHER.274.question: Projected by a broken laser pointer during physics class, solve this Complete Columnar quote from Jane Fonda. The quote has OMTOT somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.274.author: Jane Fonda

CIPHER.271.cipherString: We delight in the beauty of butterfly, rarely admit the changes it has gone through to achieve that beauty
CIPHER.271.question: You receive this puzzling text during a live science broadcast. It contains a quote by Maya Angelou, encrypted as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.271.author: Maya Angelou

CIPHER.287.cipherString: What part of your family can you see through? A transparent.
CIPHER.287.question: You stumble across this encoded text on an old floppy disk labeled “Project Discovery.” It's a quote encoded as a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.287.author: Unknown

CIPHER.266.cipherString: Truth from the mouth of an honest man and severity from a good-natured man have a double effect.
CIPHER.266.question: You find this encoded line written in invisible ink on a lab safety poster. It's a quote by Hazlitt, written in a Complete Columnar. The quote has MANAN somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.266.author: Hazlitt

CIPHER.279.cipherString: Every case stands upon its own bottom.
CIPHER.279.question: A strange code appears in the footer of a document you print from the lab computer. It's a quote, encoded in a Baconian. You are told that the deciphered text starts with EVER. What does it say?

CIPHER.264.cipherString: But man is not made for defeat. A man can be destroyed but not defeated.
CIPHER.264.question: The janitor found this strange paper strip under a Bunsen burner. It's a quote, encoded with a Nihilist Substitution Cipher with a keyword length of 6. The 10th through 21st cipher units (65 92 66 29 45 47 55 84 39 27 64 48) decode to be OTMADEFORDEF. What does it say?

CIPHER.283.cipherString: It isn't given to very many men to have their dreams come true in their lifetimes.
CIPHER.283.question: A message in a bottle washed up in the school's decorative fountain—decode this Checkerboard cipher text by Algis Budrys. The 7th through 13th cipher units (IW NG SW SN NO EG PR) decode to be GIVENTO. What does it say?
CIPHER.283.author: Algis Budrys

CIPHER.275.cipherString: The teachings tell us that a harvest is made honorable by what you give in return for what you take.
CIPHER.275.question: A mysterious balloon release at the science fair carried this Aristocrat banner. You are told that the cipher contains IN. What does it say?

CIPHER.289.cipherString: Myth therefore seems to choose history, rather than be chosen by it.
CIPHER.289.question: The school's ancient overhead projector flickered and burned this Porta message onto the screen—decode François-Bernard Mâche words using a keyword of ARGUED. What does it say?
CIPHER.289.author: François-Bernard Mâche

CIPHER.290.cipherString: Success in life is not for those who run fast, but for those who keep running and always on the move.
CIPHER.290.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by Bangambiki Habyarimana, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.290.author: Bangambiki Habyarimana
